tasks.title: Consolidate Firebase client config into shared module
tasks.description:
firebase-helpers.js (introduced in Task #6) hardcodes the Firebase client config block (apiKey, projectId, appId, etc.), duplicating what is already present in students.html, transcript.html, and dashboard.html. Firebase client config is not a secret, but duplication creates a maintenance sync burden — a project change requires updating all copies.

Extract the config into a shared module (e.g. ai-academy/js/firebase-config.js) and import it from all consumers. Verify all pages still initialize Firebase correctly after the change.

tasks.title: Build REST API
tasks.description:
The API needs to:

✅ Accept a text query (concepts extracted from video)
✅ Search Aesop Academy's course catalog
✅ Return multiple ranked courses (by relevance score)
✅ Include course title, description/blurb, URL, and metadata
✅ Be callable from a Cloud Function in the experts project
tasks.plan:
## Overview

25experts.com maps YouTube videos to Aesop Academy courses. They need a REST API endpoint that returns a catalog of courses (id, name, description, URL) plus a version hash for change detection. The API will be called periodically by their Cloud Function, which will cache the courses locally and use them for video-to-course matching.

## API Specification

Endpoint: GET https://aesopacademy.org/aesop-api/catalog.php
Response: { catalog_hash, generated_at, courses[] }
Each course: { id, name, desc, url, live }
Data source: courses-data.json (179 courses)
Versioning: SHA-256 content hash of courses-data.json
Deployment: Mocahost shared hosting via GitHub Actions FTP CI/CD

## Phases

1. Create PHP endpoint (read courses-data.json, compute hash, return JSON)
2. Configure CORS headers (allow cross-origin requests)
3. Test the endpoint (verify JSON, hash, CORS locally)
4. Deploy to Mocahost (commit, push, verify in production)

## Proof Units

See proofUnits array for detailed verification steps for each phase.

proofUnits.title: Endpoint returns valid JSON with all required fields
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: GET https://aesopacademy.org/aesop-api/catalog.php returns HTTP 200 with JSON containing catalog_hash, generated_at, and courses array with id, name, desc, url, live fields per course.
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: File does not exist (404), or returns HTML error, or JSON is malformed, or missing fields
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: HTTP 200 with valid JSON; catalog_hash is 64-char hex string; courses array has >170 elements; each course has all 5 fields
proofUnits.waiverGuidance: N/A — API response is the proof

proofUnits.title: SHA-256 hash changes when courses-data.json changes
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: After modifying courses-data.json, the endpoint's catalog_hash value changes. Reverting the file restores the original hash.
proofUnits.exactCommand: curl -s http://localhost:8000/aesop-api/catalog.php | jq '.catalog_hash' (before) → modify courses-data.json → curl again (after) → compare hashes
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: Hash does not change after modifying file, or hash is empty/null
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Hash values differ when file modified; match when reverted; at least 3 distinct values shown

proofUnits.title: CORS headers allow cross-origin requests
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Calling endpoint from different origin (curl with Origin header, or browser fetch) succeeds and returns CORS headers.
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: CORS headers missing, or Origin not allowed, or fetch fails with CORS error
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Response headers show Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *; fetch succeeds from external domain
proofUnits.waiverGuidance: N/A — CORS headers verifiable via curl or browser DevTools

proofUnits.title: Endpoint deployed to production and accessible
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: https://aesopacademy.org/aesop-api/catalog.php is accessible, returns HTTP 200 and same JSON structure as development.
proofUnits.waiverGuidance: Wait 5-10 minutes after pushing main for FTP deployment to complete

proofUnits.title: 25experts.com can call API and parse response
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: 25experts.com Cloud Function successfully calls endpoint, receives JSON, extracts and caches courses array.
proofUnits.exactCommand: Coordinate with 25experts.com team: trigger their syncVideoToCourses function, check logs for 'Fetched N courses' message, verify Firestore document written with course data
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: 25experts cannot fetch endpoint (404, 500, timeout, CORS), response not JSON, courses empty
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Cloud Function logs show successful fetch and parsing; courses >=170; Firestore documents with course data
proofUnits.waiverGuidance: Accept manual verification from 25experts.com engineer: API reachable, response valid JSON, courses populated, CORS ok, no errors
